title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By compromising CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code at scale, potentially leading to data breaches, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and multi-factor authentication (MFA) for CI/CD pipelines and cloud management interfaces.
Regularly scan and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unauthorized changes or malicious code injections.
Ensure frequent backups of critical data and store them in isolated environments to facilitate recovery in case of ransomware attacks.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: The Joomla extension Phoca Downloads is vulnerable to an authenticated arbitrary file upload that...
description: The Joomla extension Phoca Downloads is vulnerable to an authenticated arbitrary file upload that allows registered users uploading executable files and leads to full RCE.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-57828.

title: The Joomla extension RSFiles is vulnerable to an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload that...

title: The W3 Total Cache plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Directory Traversal in all versions up...
description: The W3 Total Cache plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Directory Traversal in all versions up to, and including, 2.9.4 via the setupSources function. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to read the contents of arbitrary files on the server, which can contain sensitive information. Exploitation requires enabling manual minify mode and supplying a manual-format minify filename so that the hash is empty and the f_array[] entries are not overwritten before reaching setupSources().

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the W3 Total Cache plugin allows unauthenticated attackers to exploit a directory traversal flaw, potentially exposing sensitive files on the server. This could lead to data breaches, credential theft, or further exploitation of the compromised server.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the W3 Total Cache plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable it temporarily to mitigate the risk.
Audit server logs for any signs of unauthorized access or exploitation attempts, particularly for unusual file access patterns.
Restrict access to sensitive files on the server by implementing proper file permissions and disabling manual minify mode if not required.

title: The CorvusPay WooCommerce Payment Gateway plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site...
description: The CorvusPay WooCommerce Payment Gateway plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'approval_code' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 2.7.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. The unauthenticated REST endpoint POST /wp-json/corvuspay/success/ is registered with permission_ca

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts via the 'approval_code' parameter, potentially leading to stored cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks. This could result in data theft, session hijacking, or malicious redirection for users accessing compromised pages.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the CorvusPay WooCommerce Payment Gateway plugin to a patched version (if available) or temporarily disable the plugin until a fix is released.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests targeting the vulnerable endpoint.
Conduct a security audit of your WordPress site to identify and remove any malicious scripts injected via this vulnerability.

title: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to...
description: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 9.2.2. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9017.

title: The Essential Addons for Elementor – Popular Elementor Templates & Widgets plugin for WordPress...
description: The Essential Addons for Elementor – Popular Elementor Templates & Widgets plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authenticated Account Takeover via Email Header Injection in all versions up to, and including, 6.6.10 This is due to insufficient server-side validation of a Login/Register widget setting used to construct outgoing email headers — the allowed-values restriction is enforced only in the client-side editor UI and not on the server, and the applied sanitization does not strip or encode C

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15155.

title: The fresh Podcaster plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The fresh Podcaster plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'freshpodcaster' shortcode in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.7 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping on user supplied attributes. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1382.

title: The WCFM – Frontend Manager for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization...
description: The WCFM – Frontend Manager for WooCommerce plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 6.7.27. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary reply content into any store inquiry, overwrite the main inquiry record in wp_wcfm_enquiries, and trigger unsolicited notification emails to customers and vendors. Unlike sibling co

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized attackers to manipulate customer and vendor inquiries, potentially leading to data integrity issues, phishing attacks, and reputational damage for affected businesses. Exploitation could result in unsolicited notifications, confusion, and loss of trust among users.
recommended_actions: Update the WCFM – Frontend Manager for WooCommerce plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block unauthorized access attempts.
Regularly review and audit user permissions and logs to identify and mitigate unauthorized activity.

title: The Context Blog theme for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all...
description: The Context Blog theme for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Exposure in all versions up to, and including, 1.3.5 via the context_blog_modal_popup. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to extract the content of password-protected posts.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive information, such as the content of password-protected posts. This could lead to unauthorized disclosure of confidential or private data, potentially harming individuals or organizations relying on the affected WordPress theme.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Context Blog theme to a patched or newer version if available. If no patch is available, consider switching to a more secure theme.
Restrict access to sensitive posts by implementing additional authentication mechanisms, such as HTTP Basic Authentication or IP whitelisting.
Monitor logs for unauthorized access attempts and review any potentially exposed content for sensitive information.

title: The WP CTA – Sticky CTA Builder, Generate Leads, Promote Sales plugin for WordPress is vulnerable...
description: The WP CTA – Sticky CTA Builder, Generate Leads, Promote Sales plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to time-based blind SQL Injection via the 'fildname' parameter in all versions up to, and including, 2.2.2. This is due to insufficient escaping of user-supplied column names in the ajaxCheck() method and lack of preparation in the $wpdb->update() call. The vulnerability is compounded by the complete absence of authorization checks and the endpoint being registered for unauthenticated users via wp_a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4661.

title: The bbp Style Pack plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting in versions...
description: The bbp Style Pack plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting in versions up to, and including, 6.4.5 via the Topic Form Additional Fields feature. This is due to insufficient input sanitization in bsp_topic_fields_form_save() (which writes $_POST['bsp_topic_fields_label{n}'] directly to post meta via update_post_meta() with no filtering) and missing output escaping in bsp_topic_content_append_topic_fields() (which concatenates the stored meta value into an HTML <span> and

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15010.

title: The Genolve – AI image AI video generation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized...
description: The Genolve – AI image AI video generation plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to a missing capability check on the genolve_setOpt() function in all versions up to, and including, 5.0.5. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to update arbitrary WordPress options, including enabling user registration and setting the default role to administrator, resulting in privilege escalation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1359.

title: The WP Hotel Booking plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insufficient Verification of Data...
description: The WP Hotel Booking plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity in all versions up to, and including, 2.3.1. This is due to the `web_hook_process_paypal_standard()` IPN handler selecting its PayPal validation endpoint from the attacker-controlled `$_REQUEST['test_ipn']` parameter, force-upgrading any `pending` transaction to `completed` when `test_ipn=1`, and omitting post-verification checks on `receiver_email`, `mc_currency`, and `txn_id` uniqueness af

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to manipulate the PayPal IPN handler to mark pending transactions as completed without proper verification. This could lead to financial fraud, unauthorized access to services, and loss of revenue for businesses using the plugin.
affected_industries: Small Businesses using WordPress for bookings
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WP Hotel Booking plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily until a fix is released.
Implement additional server-side validation for PayPal IPN responses, including verifying 'receiver_email', 'mc_currency', and 'txn_id' for uniqueness.
Monitor transaction logs for suspicious activity and unauthorized changes to booking statuses.

title: The White Label CMS plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via admin...
description: The White Label CMS plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via admin settings in all versions up to, and including, 2.7.12 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level permissions and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. This only affects multi-site installations and installations where unfiltered_html has b

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with administrator-level permissions to inject malicious scripts into WordPress pages, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or further exploitation of users accessing the affected pages. This is particularly concerning for multi-site installations where multiple sites could be compromised.
recommended_actions: Update the White Label CMS plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict administrator-level access to trusted personnel only and review user permissions on multi-site installations.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts and monitor for unusual activity.

title: CVE-2025-71372 - Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle __r
description: Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle __reduce__ methods, allowing arbitrary code execution. Attackers can craft malicious pickle files that execute arbitrary Python code when loaded, bypassing Picklescan's safety checks and enabling supply-chain poisoning of shared model files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary Python code by exploiting unsafe pickle deserialization, potentially leading to supply-chain attacks, data breaches, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Picklescan to version 0.0.33 or later to ensure detection of the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget.
Implement strict validation and avoid loading untrusted pickle files in applications.
Educate development teams on the risks of deserializing untrusted data and enforce secure coding practices.

title: CVE-2025-71369 - picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data.datapipes.
description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data.datapipes.utils.decoder.basichandlers in reduce methods, allowing attackers to bypass safety checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected malicious code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71369.

title: CVE-2025-71367 - picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect _operator.attrgetter function calls in pickle payloads, all
description: picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect _operator.attrgetter function calls in pickle payloads, allowing attackers to bypass security checks. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle files using _operator.attrgetter in reduce methods to execute arbitrary code when pickle.load() processes the file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in picklescan before version 0.0.34 allows attackers to execute arbitrary code by bypassing security checks. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or system compromise, especially in applications that rely on untrusted or user-supplied pickle files.
recommended_actions: Upgrade picklescan to version 0.0.34 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Avoid using pickle to process untrusted or user-supplied data, and consider safer serialization alternatives like JSON or protobuf.
Implement strict input validation and sandboxing mechanisms to limit the impact of malicious payloads.

title: CVE-2025-71366 - picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious torch.utils.bottleneck.__main__.run_cprofile func
description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious torch.utils.bottleneck.__main__.run_cprofile function calls in pickle files, allowing attackers to bypass safety checks. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71366.

title: CVE-2026-54424 - An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potent
description: An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potential Elevation of Privilege. This issue affects Parsec through v2026-05-04.0. The patched version is Parsec for Windows version 150-104a. A user can generate a situation where there is an instance of parsecd.exe running as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM with a user-controlled value of the AppData environment variable.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54424.
